name: session-improver
description: Optimize your Cursor AI workflow by analyzing session history to identify inefficiencies. Detects token waste from repeated file reads, linter loops from unfixed errors, and missing automations. Recommends hooks, linter configs, and workflow improvements. Use '/improve-session' or run 'parse-session --current' to analyze your current session.
longDescription:
This plugin parses Cursor's SQLite session database to find patterns that waste tokens and time. It identifies:
- Repeated file reads that could be consolidated
- Linter errors that repeatedly appear without fixes
- Missing hooks that could automate manual tasks
- Workflow patterns that could be improved with skills

Provides actionable recommendations with specific commands and configurations to improve your development efficiency.
features: Session history analysis from SQLite database
usageExamples: parse-session --current - CLI analysis of current session
